programs.name: Rhode Island Medicaid
programs.what_it_does: Provides comprehensive healthcare coverage including long-term care services for eligible seniors and individuals with disabilities. Administered by the Executive Office of Health and Human Services (EOHHS), it covers doctor visits, hospital stays, prescription drugs, and home-based care.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ and individuals with disabilities meeting income and asset limits.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers full cost of approved medical services with no or low copays.

programs.name: Home and Community-Based Services (HCBS) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Offers waiver services to allow seniors and disabled individuals to receive care at home instead of nursing facilities. Includes personal care, homemaker services, and adult day care funded through Medicaid.
programs.who_its_for: Medicaid-eligible seniors and disabled persons at risk of nursing home placement.
programs.benefit_value: Annual funding limits based on assessed needs; prevents institutionalization.

programs.name: PACE-RI (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings including adult day health centers. Integrates Medicare and Medicaid benefits for coordinated care.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 55+ certified as nursing facility eligible who can live safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of primary, acute, and long-term care services.

programs.name: Medicare Premium Payment Program (MPP) / QMB
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare Part A and B premiums, deductibles, copayments, and coinsurance for eligible low-income seniors. As of 2026, expanded to automatically enroll former SLMB/QI eligible into QMB.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries; income up to 100-135% FPL depending on category.
programs.benefit_value: Full payment of Medicare premiums (Part B ~$185/month in 2026) and cost-sharing.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly EBT card benefits to purchase healthy groceries at approved stores. Seniors can access simplified application, phone interviews, and 24-month recertification.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+; gross income ≤185% FPL, net ≤100% FPL.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per senior household; max benefits scale with income[1][7].

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides grants to help pay heating costs during winter months. Offers crisis assistance for utility shutoffs and weatherization referrals.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors; priority for 60+ and disabled.

programs.name: Residential Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Funds energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, air sealing, and furnace repairs for low-income homes. Reduces energy costs and improves health/safety.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners/renters including seniors; income ≤200% FPL.
programs.benefit_value: Free services valued at $7,000-12,000 per home.

programs.name: RI SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free, unbiased counseling on Medicare options, supplemental insurance, and assistance programs. Helps with applications, appeals, and understanding coverage.
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries and caregivers in Rhode Island.
programs.benefit_value: Free personalized counseling; average savings $1,000+/year through proper enrollment.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors' doors daily. Provides safety checks and links to other services through local senior centers.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals; suggested donation basis.
programs.benefit_value: $8-12 per meal; often free for low-income.

programs.name: Alzheimer's Respite Voucher Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides vouchers for respite care services allowing caregivers temporary breaks. Covers in-home care, adult day programs, or overnight stays.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of RI residents with Alzheimer's/dementia.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $27/hour vouchers; annual cap $3,600 per caregiver.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time community service work training leading to unsubsidized employment. Pays minimum wage for 20 hours/week at nonprofit/community sites.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ lacking skills for self-support.
programs.benefit_value: $15/hour wage (RI minimum); work experience and job placement.

programs.name: Rhode Island Legal Services (RILS) Elder Law Unit
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance for income-eligible seniors on housing, benefits, consumer issues, and abuse prevention. Offers advice, representation, and community education.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with income ≤125% FPL and priority legal problems.
programs.benefit_value: Free legal services valued at $300+/hour attorney rate.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes, assisted living, and other long-term care facilities. Investigates complaints, resolves issues, and promotes quality of life.
programs.who_its_for: All residents and families in RI long-term care facilities.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy; resolves 80%+ of complaints without litigation.

programs.name: Rhode Island Pharmaceutical Assistance Program for the Elderly (RIPAE)
programs.what_it_does: RIPAE provides financial assistance to eligible seniors for purchasing prescription drugs not covered by Medicare. It helps reduce out-of-pocket costs for medications essential to health management.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Rhode Island seniors aged 65+ with limited income and assets, beyond standard federal Medicare coverage.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded pharmaceutical aid specifically tailored for RI elderly, supplementing federal programs like Medicare Part D.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by income; covers copays and premiums up to state-set limits, potentially thousands annually in drug cost savings.

programs.name: At Home Cost Share Program
programs.what_it_does: This program offers case management and coordinated access to long-term services and supports (LTSS) to help seniors remain safely at home. It partners with local agencies for personalized care planning.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Eligible seniors in Newport County and Providence with limited income, often in partnership with RI Office of Healthy Aging.
programs.whats_unique: RI-specific state-funded initiative for home-based case management, distinct from federal Medicaid waivers.
programs.benefit_value: Free case management services; connects to LTSS worth thousands in home care support annually.

programs.name: Ocean State Senior Dining Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides nutritious meals at congregate dining sites for seniors to promote health and social engagement. It supports nutrition access for those facing food insecurity.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Rhode Island seniors aged 60+ who are homebound or attend senior centers.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific congregate meal program branded for RI, expanding beyond federal Meals on Wheels.
programs.benefit_value: Free or low-cost meals (up to 5 per week), valued at $10-15 per meal.

programs.name: RIPTA Senior Reduced Fare Bus Pass
programs.what_it_does: Offers discounted fares on all Rhode Island Public Transit Authority (RIPTA) buses for senior riders. Enables affordable access to errands, medical appointments, and recreation statewide.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Rhode Island residents aged 65 and older.
programs.whats_unique: Statewide reduced fare pass unique to RI's public transit system, not a federal program.
programs.benefit_value: $1 fare (half of regular $2); annual pass options available for frequent users.
